title: quercus stellata subsp. paludosa
intro: Quercus stellata subsp. paludosa, also known as the swamp white oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree that belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae). The bark and leaves of the Quercus stellata subsp. paludosa contain tannins, which have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus stellata subsp. paludosa are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antioxidant. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
